questions.text: Which of these is a rational number?
questions.explanation: 0 is a rational number as it can be written as 0/1. √2, π, √3 are irrational numbers.

questions.text: Between any two rational numbers, there are infinitely many rational numbers.
questions.explanation: Between any two rational numbers, there exist infinitely many rational numbers. This is a key property of rational numbers.

questions.text: What is the reciprocal of a rational number?
questions.correctAnswer: The number obtained by interchanging numerator and denominator.
questions.explanation: Reciprocal of a/b is b/a. Product of a number and its reciprocal is 1.

questions.text: The additive identity for rational numbers is ________.
questions.explanation: 0 is the additive identity because adding 0 to any rational number gives the same number.
